source: Kapoor & Sons (2016) — full screenplay; weighted to the 'perfect family' / family-photograph motif, the favoured-son fracture ('Mera perfect baccha'), the dinner-table blow-up and Dadu's wish
love_type: cult — critically adored and deeply loved by audiences who saw their own families in it; a quiet, rewatched favourite rather than a slogan blockbuster
core_emotion: the bittersweet recognition of your own imperfect family — love and resentment tangled together, and the longing to be seen and accepted as you are

dialogue_style.register: lived-in, overlapping naturalistic family Hindi-English — interruptions, old grudges, in-jokes; nobody speechifies
dialogue_style.rhythm: everyday domestic cross-talk that suddenly curdles into long-buried accusations
dialogue_style.stylization: grounded realism — deliberately un-Bollywood; emotion through accumulation, not declaration
dialogue_style.why_it's_theirs: the refusal of melodrama is the signature — the love lands precisely because the dialogue sounds like a real family at dinner, not a movie

signature_dialogues.construction: a mother's casual term of endearment that quietly encodes favouritism — two words that name the whole family's fracture
signature_dialogues.the_setup: the 'good' son Rahul is openly preferred; the line lands the parental partiality that has warped both brothers
signature_dialogues.presentation: dropped tenderly, almost throwaway, in an ordinary moment — the lack of drama is what makes it cut, because the audience sees the wound the speaker doesn't
signature_dialogues.payoff: the word 'perfect' sets up the film's whole irony: there is no perfect child and no perfect family, only the myth that damages them
signature_dialogues.why_quotable: anyone from a family with a favoured sibling feels it instantly — quietly devastating recognition

signature_dialogues.line: the wish for one 'perfect family photograph' (Dadu's recurring desire)
signature_dialogues.construction: a concrete object (a single complete family photo) as the emblem of an impossible ideal
signature_dialogues.the_setup: the grandfather just wants one photo with everyone together and happy; secrets keep sabotaging it
signature_dialogues.presentation: the photo attempt recurs as a motif and pays off only after death and rupture — the ideal is reached imperfectly, which is the point; the staging makes a mundane family ritual unbearably poignant
signature_dialogues.payoff: the 'perfect' photo is finally taken only once the family accepts it can't be perfect — acceptance as the real completion
signature_dialogues.why_quotable: the 'perfect family photo' became shorthand for the gap between the families we perform and the ones we have

the_peaks.moment: the dinner-table explosion where every buried secret and grievance erupts at once
the_peaks.why_loved: the most honest family fight in modern Hindi cinema; viewers see their own dinners

the_peaks.moment: Dadu's death and the finally-taken imperfect family photograph
the_peaks.why_loved: acceptance and grief arriving together — catharsis through imperfection, not resolution

wish_fulfillment: to be loved and accepted by your family exactly as you are — flaws, secrets, sexuality and all — without having to be 'perfect'
character_magnetism: Dadu's mischievous warmth as the family's beating heart; the ache of Rahul carrying a secret and Arjun carrying resentment
audacity: centering a gay son's quiet reality without sensationalizing it, and refusing tidy reconciliation — letting the family stay imperfect
portability: the 'perfect family' / family-photo motif as a widely-used metaphor for performed vs real family life
why_cult_not_just_good: audiences adopt it as the film they cite about their OWN families — personal, repeat-watched, emotionally claimed
transferable_love_triggers: Hide the central wound inside a casual term of endearment ('perfect child') so the audience sees the damage the character doesn't — quiet cuts deepest.
Use one concrete object (a family photograph) as the emblem of an impossible ideal, and pay it off imperfectly — acceptance beats resolution.
Build the emotional peak from accumulated everyday grievances erupting at once, not a single villain — real-family honesty is the love-trigger.
Refuse melodrama and tidy reconciliation; let the family stay imperfect, because recognition ('that's MY family') is more lovable than a fix.
Treat 'perfect' as the enemy — name the myth early so the whole story can dismantle it and reward acceptance.
confidence: high on theme/motif (grounded) and reception; specific photo-line wording paraphrased from the film's well-known motif
